name: Community Services Of Nevada (Csnv)
description_short: Nevada non-profit offering free HUD-approved housing counseling, foreclosure intervention, and financial education to help residents with homebuying, rental assistance, and homeownership decisions.
description_long:
Community Services Of Nevada (CSNV) is a community development non-profit organization based in Nevada dedicated to providing essential financial and housing services to residents. The organization operates with a mission to change lives for the better through comprehensive counseling and education programs. Founded to serve underserved populations, CSNV addresses critical gaps in financial literacy and housing access across the state.

CSNV offers a comprehensive suite of free counseling services including homebuyer assistance, foreclosure intervention, post-purchase mortgage counseling, and rental services. Their homebuyer program guides prospective buyers through credit counseling, budgeting, savings strategies, mortgage processes, and down payment assistance programs. They provide foreclosure intervention counseling to help homeowners understand alternatives and avoid losing their homes. Additionally, CSNV offers free rental counseling and budgeting services for renters, plus low-cost immigration services and limited-time tax preparation services. All core housing counseling services are available through one-on-one in-person counseling, online courses, free webinars, and appointment-based sessions.

CSNV distinguishes itself through free, individual counseling appointments rather than group sessions, with counselors available via phone (702-307-1710), email, text, and online booking systems. They explicitly focus on preventing predatory lending and mortgage scams by educating borrowers before they commit to loans. The organization offers both foundational financial education and specialized post-purchase counseling, serving the complete homeownership lifecycle. Their multi-service approach addresses housing, immigration, and tax needs within a single organization.

As a legitimate non-profit housing counselor, CSNV is appropriate for Nevada residents genuinely seeking free financial education and housing guidance. The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are Nevada-specific. The organization's website provides clear contact information and appointment scheduling, indicating operational legitimacy. However, clients should verify current service availability, as some offerings are described as available "during a limited time through the year" (particularly tax services).
pros: Completely free housing counseling services with no hidden fees or commercial lending agenda
Individual one-on-one counseling appointments available, not just group classes or automated guidance
Explicit focus on preventing predatory lending and mortgage fraud before clients commit to loans
Multi-format learning options: in-person classes, online courses, free webinars, and text/phone support
Counselors guide through complete homebuying journey from credit counseling through down payment assistance programs
Foreclosure intervention services available with orientations two days per week
Post-purchase counseling specifically covers refinancing to prevent mortgage fraud
Easy appointment scheduling and multiple contact methods (phone, email, text, web form)
cons: Limited geographic scope—services only available to Nevada residents
Some services (tax preparation) available only during limited periods of the year, creating scheduling uncertainty
Website does not specify counselor credentials, HUD-approval status, or whether counselors are NFCC-certified despite serving as free-help provider
No information about current waitlists, average counseling duration, or response time for appointment requests
Immigration and tax services are low-cost rather than free, requiring separate evaluation of those service fees
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Nevada seeking free pre-purchase education and mortgage process guidance
Nevada homeowners facing foreclosure who need to understand alternatives and restructuring options
Renters in Nevada looking for budgeting assistance and guidance on rental affordability
Homeowners considering refinancing who want to understand risks before committing to new mortgage terms
diagnosis: CSNV is best for Nevada residents who are first-time homebuyers, facing foreclosure, or seeking rental guidance and want free, personalized counseling from a non-profit rather than commercial lenders. The main caveat is that services are Nevada-specific only, and prospective clients should contact the organization directly to confirm current availability and counselor credentials before relying on their guidance for major financial decisions.
services: Free homebuyer counseling and education (credit counseling, budgeting, savings, mortgage process)
Down payment assistance program information and guidance
Foreclosure intervention counseling with free orientation sessions
Free webinars on down payment assistance and home buying
Low-cost immigration services (citizenship, permanent residency, status renewal)
Youth education programs and adult education programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 500 N Rainbow Blvd, Las Vegas, NV 89107
